recentActivity.description: Welcome to your AI Placement Preparation Assistant! Start by adding your Gemini API key in Settings or exploring the DSA section.

dsaProblems.title: Two Sum
dsaProblems.description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

You can return the answer in any order.
dsaProblems.constraints:
• 2 <= nums.length <= 10^4
• -10^9 <= nums[i] <= 10^9
• -10^9 <= target <= 10^9
• Only one valid answer exists.

dsaProblems.title: Reverse a String
dsaProblems.description:
Write a function that reverses a string. The input string is given as an array of characters `s`.

You must do this by modifying the input array in-place with O(1) extra memory.
dsaProblems.constraints:
• 1 <= s.length <= 10^5
• s[i] is a printable ascii character.

dsaProblems.title: Valid Parentheses
dsaProblems.description:
Given a string `s` containing just the characters `'('`, `')'`, `'{'`, `'}'`, `'['` and `']'`, determine if the input string is valid.

An input string is valid if:
1. Open brackets must be closed by the same type of brackets.
2. Open brackets must be closed in the correct order.
3. Every close bracket has a corresponding open bracket of the same type.
dsaProblems.constraints:
• 1 <= s.length <= 10^4
• s consists of parentheses only '()[]{}'.

dsaProblems.title: Fibonacci Number
dsaProblems.description:
The Fibonacci numbers, commonly denoted `F(n)` form a sequence, called the Fibonacci sequence, such that each number is the sum of the two preceding ones, starting from 0 and 1.

F(0) = 0, F(1) = 1
F(n) = F(n - 1) + F(n - 2), for n > 1.

Given `n`, calculate `F(n)`.

quantitative.question: A train running at the speed of 60 km/hr crosses a pole in 9 seconds. What is the length of the train?
quantitative.explanation:
Speed = 60 km/hr = 60 * (5/18) m/s = 50/3 m/s.
Time taken = 9 seconds.
Length of the train = Speed * Time = (50/3) * 9 = 150 metres.

quantitative.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 12 days, B and C in 15 days, and C and A in 20 days. In how many days can A alone finish the work?
quantitative.explanation:
(A+B)'s 1 day work = 1/12
(B+C)'s 1 day work = 1/15
(C+A)'s 1 day work = 1/20
Adding these: 2(A+B+C)'s 1 day work = 1/12 + 1/15 + 1/20 = (5+4+3)/60 = 12/60 = 1/5
(A+B+C)'s 1 day work = 1/10.
A's 1 day work = (A+B+C)'s 1 day work - (B+C)'s 1 day work = 1/10 - 1/15 = (3-2)/30 = 1/30.
Hence, A alone can finish the work in 30 days.

logical.question: Look at this series: 2, 1, (1/2), (1/4), ... What number should come next?
logical.explanation:
This is a simple division series. Each number is one-half of the previous number.
The next term is (1/4) / 2 = 1/8.

logical.question: If in a certain language, CHARCOAL is coded as 45164913 and MORAL is coded as 29613, how is ALLY coded in that language?
logical.explanation:
Matching individual letters to numbers:
C=4, H=5, A=1, R=6, O=9, L=3, M=2, Y=6 (Wait, from the prompt choices: A=1, L=3, L=3, Y=6 is 1336. Let's see: L matches to 3, A matches to 1, Y matches to 6. Thus, ALLY is 1336).

verbal.question: Choose the word which is closest in meaning (synonym) to: 'ADVERSITY'
verbal.explanation: Adversity refers to a state or instance of serious or continued difficulty or misfortune. Therefore, 'Misfortune' is the synonym.

verbal.question: Identify the grammatically correct sentence:
options: Everyone of the students have completed their assignments.
Every one of the students has completed his or her assignment.
Everyone of the student has completed their assignment.
Every student have completed his assignment.
verbal.explanation: 'Every one' is singular and takes a singular verb ('has') and singular pronoun ('his or her').
